name: troubleshooting
context: Debug Cloudflare Workers errors, deployment failures, binding issues, and platform limits
info: This node covers common errors, debugging strategies, and platform limits across the Cloudflare developer platform. Workers have specific constraints: 1MB script size (free), 10MB (paid), 10ms CPU time (free), 30s (paid), 128MB memory, no Node.js built-in modules (use compatibility flags). Common failures include binding misconfiguration, wrangler.toml errors, authentication failures, and hitting rate or size limits. The primary debugging tools are `wrangler tail` for live log streaming, `wrangler dev` for local development, and the Workers dashboard for error analytics.
triggers: my cloudflare worker is not working
body:
# Troubleshooting Cloudflare

## Common Errors

### 1. Script Too Large (1101)

```
Error: Script startup exceeded CPU time limit
```

Workers have size limits: 1MB compressed (free), 10MB compressed (paid). Solutions:
- Tree-shake unused dependencies
- Use `wrangler deploy --minify`
- Move large data to KV or R2 instead of bundling
- Split into multiple Workers with Service Bindings

### 2. CPU Time Exceeded (1102)

```
Error: Worker exceeded CPU time limit
```

Free plan: 10ms CPU time. Paid: 30s CPU time (not wall-clock — waiting for I/O doesn't count). Solutions:
- Offload heavy computation to a Durable Object or external service
- Use `waitUntil()` for non-critical background work
- Cache expensive computations in [[cloudflare/kv]]

### 3. Memory Limit (1103)

Workers have 128MB memory limit. Solutions:
- Stream large responses instead of buffering
- Process data in chunks
- Use [[cloudflare/r2]] for large objects instead of in-memory processing

### 4. Binding Not Found

```
Error: No binding named 'MY_KV' found
```

Binding is missing from `wrangler.toml` or environment doesn't match. Check:
- Binding name in `wrangler.toml` matches the code reference
- Correct environment section (production vs preview)
- Run `wrangler deploy` after adding bindings

### 5. Authentication Errors (9109, 10000)

```json
{"success":false,"errors":[{"code":10000,"message":"Authentication error"}]}
```

Solutions:
- Verify `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` is set and not expired
- Check token has required permissions. See [[cloudflare/auth#permission-sets]]
- Ensure `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` is correct
- Run `wrangler whoami` to verify credentials

### 6. Subrequest Limit

Workers can make up to 50 subrequests (fetch calls) per invocation (free) or 1000 (paid). Solutions:
- Batch API calls where possible
- Use Durable Objects for coordination
- Cache responses in [[cloudflare/kv]] to reduce repeated fetches

## Debugging Tools

### wrangler tail

Stream live logs from a deployed Worker:

```bash
# Stream all logs
wrangler tail my-worker

# Filter by status
wrangler tail my-worker --status error

# Filter by search string
wrangler tail my-worker --search "TypeError"

# JSON output for piping
wrangler tail my-worker --format json
```

### wrangler dev

Local development with hot reload:

```bash
# Start local dev server
wrangler dev

# With remote resources (KV, R2, D1)
wrangler dev --remote

# Specify port
wrangler dev --port 8787
```

### Console Logging

```javascript
export default {
  async fetch(request, env) {
    console.log('Request URL:', request.url);
    console.log('Request method:', request.method);
    console.log('KV binding exists:', !!env.MY_KV);
    // Logs appear in wrangler tail and wrangler dev
  }
};
```

## Platform Limits

| Resource | Free | Paid |
|----------|------|------|
| Requests/day | 100,000 | 10M included, then $0.50/M |
| CPU time/request | 10ms | 30s |
| Script size | 1MB | 10MB |
| Memory | 128MB | 128MB |
| Subrequests | 50 | 1,000 |
| Environment variables | 64 | 128 |
| KV reads/day | 100,000 | $0.50/M |
| KV writes/day | 1,000 | $5.00/M |
| KV value size | 25MB | 25MB |
| R2 storage | 10GB | $0.015/GB/mo |
| D1 rows read/day | 5M | $0.001/M |
| D1 rows written/day | 100K | $1.00/M |
| D1 database size | 500MB | 10GB |

sections.title: Common error codes
sections.body: 1101: Script too large. 1102: CPU time exceeded. 1103: Memory limit. 9109/10000: Authentication error. Check [[cloudflare/auth]] for credential setup. Binding errors (no binding named X) mean `wrangler.toml` is missing the binding or the environment doesn't match.

sections.title: Debugging tools
sections.body: `wrangler tail <worker>` streams live logs from production. `wrangler dev` runs locally with hot reload. `wrangler dev --remote` uses real KV/R2/D1 bindings. `console.log()` in Worker code appears in both tail and dev output. The Workers dashboard shows error rates, CPU time, and request analytics.

sections.title: Platform limits reference
sections.body: Free plan: 100K requests/day, 10ms CPU, 1MB script, 128MB memory, 50 subrequests. Paid: 10M requests/month included, 30s CPU, 10MB script, 128MB memory, 1000 subrequests. KV: 25MB max value, 100K reads/day free. R2: 10GB free storage, zero egress. D1: 500MB free, 5M reads/day free.

related.note: Wrangler provides the primary debugging tools (tail, dev)
Most authentication errors are token misconfiguration
